## [00:00 - 03:00]

All right, so the customer thank you flow. Again, this was the third of the three that I mentioned you have to do right now today. If you don't have a customer thank you, you're missing out on a lot of opportunity. The first is the opportunity to reduce buyers from mores. So again, while people are waiting for your product, they might be wondering, you know, is it in my budget? All these different things that go through their mind. The customer thank you and all these other post purchase emails are also a great way to build brand loyalty and also have great brand advocates. And again, you're doing all this to have happy customers, which lead to repeat customers, higher lifetime value and higher average order value. If thank you emails on average have about 352% higher open rate than your campaign emails because people are expecting these type of emails after they've purchased. They're ready and they're waiting to receive this. So there's a couple ways to set up this customer thank you email. One way that some of our clients like to set it up is depending on how many times someone's ordered. So I'll have a first time thank you for first time buyers and they'll have a repeat thank you for repeat buyers. So depending on how you want to set it up, you can set it up like that. But I'll show you examples of both how some clients do that and other clients just jump right into it and send everyone the same content. Customer thank you emails are also a great way to recommend other products. And you can also even provide a discount in your thank you email if it feels appropriate. So with this email I'm going to show you, this client likes to break it up based off a new customer and a repeat customer. And here are kind of a high level stats on my screen that you can see. So again once someone places an order after one day we'll send them this email and again they'll get one email on the left hand side if they're a new customer and they'll get a different email on the right hand side if they're a repeat customer. And you can see the new customer has about a 33% open rate and the repeat customer has a 51% open rate. And again both these emails have driven about $200 in revenue even though the goal there wasn't to drive money it's just to be appreciative. So this conditional split is basically saying that if someone has not opened that first email let's resend it and we're going to resend it with a different subject line. ## [03:00 - 05:28]

we wanted to thank you so much for your first purchase from us if you have any feedback or just want to say hi please send us an email and also here are our social handles you know please follow us and please tag us. And then the second email in this flow is around saying thank you again and offering them a discount code so in this case we're giving them 10% off and we're saying that discount code is good for 30 days and the call to action is to start shopping. So again great way to drive some extra revenue from people that you've shown gratitude for. So we're now diving into the repeat customer side which again is very similar in structure in the format it just has a little bit different message. The message which says you just made our day and goes into hey it feels great that you purchased from us you know again it means the world to us thank you so much. And the second email is very similar you know as a token of our gratitude for being a loyal customer please enjoy 10% off start shopping today. Again so I went through this example a little bit earlier and this is kind of a combination of a thank you email as well as kind of a product how to. This is the email from Common Blanket won't go into it too much but again tells you how you can use it thanks you for purchasing let you know that the order is going to be arriving soon and gives you the chance to kind of learn more about the brand and what customers have to say. Another example I have is from a company called Swanky Badger and this is a note from their founder Mark so he sends them an email saying I wanted to write you a personal thank you and I started the business back in 2014 and I wanted to do it to create love for people and he gives them a discount code and says thank you. So again this is a really kind of clean simple example that you can get started on today to make sure your customers start feeling appreciated. Then one last final example you can see this looks like an email that you send to a friend or a family member and this email actually works really well because it feels very personal. So the subject line is thank you again and it addresses person by name says thank you and it feels again that it was written kind of from someone's iPhone so this is really a great way to start getting your content live without having to do a whole lot of design. Your action item for this lesson is to go create the customer thank you. A great way to get started is pretend that you're writing a thank you to a friend, start typing and then it doesn't have to be much more complicated than that. It can be super simple plain text just get this out there and started. If you're not already sending this it's a huge way to connect with them. If you're already sending them this email and it feels too branded maybe tone it back and make it more personal.
